message:
feat(core): Add unknown amino acid 'X' to type system

Extend the amino acid type system to include 'X' for representing unknown
or ambiguous amino acid residues that can occur during codon translation
when encountering non-standard or incomplete codons.

Changes:
- types.ts: Add 'X' to the AminoAcid union type alongside the 20 standard
  amino acids and the stop codon '*'. Comment updated to reflect the addition.

- codons.ts: Add 'X' entry to AMINO_ACIDS record with:
  - letter: 'X' (single-letter code)
  - threeCode: '???' (distinct placeholder three-letter code)
  - name: 'Unknown' (human-readable name)
  - property: 'special' (grouped with non-standard residues)

- themes.ts: Add color definitions for 'X' amino acid across all 8 themes:
  - Classic: gray (#6c757d) - neutral indicator
  - Ocean: dark gray (#495057) - subtle contrast
  - Matrix: dark green-black (#006600/#000800) - fits the terminal aesthetic
  - Sunset: muted purple-gray (#4a4e69) - warm neutral
  - Forest: olive gray (#6b705c) - natural earth tone
  - Cyberpunk: dark purple-gray (#2a2a3a) - neon-adjacent
  - Pastel: light gray (#dfe6e9) - soft distinction
  - Monochrome: medium gray (#555555) - clear grayscale

This enables proper rendering of translated sequences containing ambiguous
positions without breaking the type system or theme color lookups.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
